2. Iterate through $nums1$.
3. If $nums1[i]$ is odd, update $O_{min} = \min(O_{min}, nums1[i])$.
4. If $nums1[i]$ is even, update $E_{min} = \min(E_{min}, nums1[i])$.
5. Check if both $O_{min}$ and $E_{min}$ were updated (i.e., both types exist).

import java.util.Arrays;

public static boolean solve(long[] nums1) {
    long oMin = Long.MAX_VALUE;
    long eMin = Long.MAX_VALUE;
    boolean hasOdd = false;
    boolean hasEven = false;

    for (long num : nums1) {
        if (num % 2 != 0) { // Odd
            hasOdd = true;
            if (num < oMin) {
                oMin = num;
            }
        } else { // Even
            hasEven = true;
            if (num < eMin) {
                eMin = num;
            }
        }
    }

    // Case 1: All same parity
    if (!hasOdd || !hasEven) {
        // If all are odd, we choose nums2[i] = nums1[i] (all odd). True.
        // If all are even, we choose nums2[i] = nums1[i] (all even). True.
        return true;
    }

    // Case 2: Mixed parities. 
    // T=0 (All Even) is impossible because O_min cannot be converted to even.
    // T=1 (All Odd) is possible iff every E_k can be converted to odd.
    // This requires E_min > O_min.
    
    // Since we are in Case 2, both hasOdd and hasEven are true.
    return eMin > oMin;
}
